title: alnus hirsuta f. inokumae
intro: Alnus hirsuta f. inokumae, also known as Japanese alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Japan, characterized by its distinctive hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Alnus hirsuta f. inokumae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
